message:
docs: refresh project documentation and expand CLI validation tests

Update README, FEATURE_PARITY, PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE, and porting plan
with current implementation state. Extend CLI model validation tests
(+57 lines). Improve main.rs dispatch logic. Rotate stale beads history
and sync tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -96,7 +96,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4","title":"Audit and close export/pages/workspace integration parity gaps","description":"Background:
Legacy `bv` is more than robot JSON and a TUI. It also includes export hooks, workspace-aware behavior, pages export and preview flows, and operator-facing deployment workflows that can quietly diverge when path semantics or bundle contracts differ.

Goal:
Rebuild confidence in the integration surfaces by auditing and then closing gaps in static export artifacts, preview/watch behavior, pages wizard flows, workspace aggregation, and hook execution semantics.

Acceptance:
- Export artifacts and runtime bundle semantics are explicitly checked against legacy expectations.
- Preview/watch/pages workflows are validated for both happy-path and failure-path behavior.
- Workspace and repo-path semantics are proven consistent across integration surfaces.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.154964889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.149035060Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","pages","parity","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.154964889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":130,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Integration track guidance:
Treat path-resolution semantics as a recurring root-cause category. Recent bugs in hooks, feedback storage, preview serving, and wizard repair flow all came from seemingly minor cwd/repo/workspace assumptions. Audit those assumptions first, then fix bundle/runtime behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","title":"Re-audit static export bundle semantics and viewer-runtime contracts","description":"Purpose:
Audit the exported bundle as a product artifact: asset inventory, bootstrap payloads, offline/static-host assumptions, CSP/cache behavior, SQLite or JSON sidecars, and viewer startup expectations.

Why this matters:
A pages export that exists but does not behave like the legacy artifact is not real parity.

Done means:
We have a concrete list of matching and missing bundle semantics, plus any required fix tasks.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:45.937867751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T21:54:49.983694804Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bundle","export","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:32.256517849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:45.937867751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","title":"Re-audit preview/watch/pages wizard workflows including failure-path operator experience","description":"Purpose:
Audit the end-to-end pages workflow: export, preview, live reload, watch loops, deploy-target configuration, saved config repair, and wizard transcripts or operator guidance. Failure paths matter as much as happy paths.

Acceptance:
The bead identifies where the workflow is equivalent, where it is weaker, and which failures still confuse or strand the operator.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.282086233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:00:48.249080763Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pages","parity","preview","watch","wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:32.925253621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.282086233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","title":"Re-audit workspace, hook, and repo-path semantics across all integration surfaces","description":"Purpose:
Many recently fixed bugs came from path-resolution assumptions. This audit must look for the same class of issue across exports, hooks, feedback/config files, workspace loading, watch paths, preview serving, and related operational flows.

Done means:
Path semantics are treated as a first-class parity surface with explicit evidence and follow-up tasks.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.639260766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T04:36:13.705489320Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hooks","parity","paths","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:33.596122596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.639260766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","title":"Re-audit workspace, hook, and repo-path semantics across all integration surfaces","description":"Purpose:
Many recently fixed bugs came from path-resolution assumptions. This audit must look for the same class of issue across exports, hooks, feedback/config files, workspace loading, watch paths, preview serving, and related operational flows.

Done means:
Path semantics are treated as a first-class parity surface with explicit evidence and follow-up tasks.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.639260766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T03:28:39.298650500Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hooks","parity","paths","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:33.596122596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.639260766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":145,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"BrightSpire","text":"Audited baseline/drift path semantics and fixed a real workspace-root mismatch.

Problem: --baseline-info, --save-baseline, --check-drift, and --robot-drift were still using raw --repo-path (or .) instead of the resolved load target/project directory. When --repo-path pointed inside a repo that should auto-promote to a workspace root via .bv/workspace.yaml, baseline files were read/written under the nested repo instead of the workspace root.

Fixes landed:
- src/main.rs now routes all four baseline/drift entry points through project_dir_for_load_target(&cli)
- workspace_discovery_start_points() now normalizes relative --repo-path values against current_dir before dedup/discovery, preventing false ambiguity between relative and absolute views of the same workspace root

Evidence/tests:
- added unit test: resolve_issue_load_target_discovers_workspace_from_relative_repo_path_without_ambiguity
- added integration test: baseline_commands_use_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace
- verification passed via rch: cargo test --test cli_model_validation baseline_commands_use_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets
- cargo fmt --check still fails repo-wide on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice; touched files were rustfmt-formatted locally","created_at":"2026-03-15T03:28:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","title":"Implement the highest-value integration fixes found by export/pages/workspace audit","description":"Purpose:
Implementation bucket for concrete export/pages/workspace mismatches uncovered by the fresh audit. Use this bead to track real code fixes, not speculative improvements.

Acceptance:
Each landed fix includes targeted regression tests and preserves deterministic operator behavior under explicit `--repo-path`, workspace-root, and non-repo cwd invocation scenarios.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.990920814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.702204761Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","implementation","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.990920814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:34.305098979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:35.087430954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:35.773187319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.1","title":"Fix static export bundle and viewer-runtime mismatches surfaced by the integration audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete fixes from `bd-2bpl.4.1` for export artifact parity: asset inventory, bootstrap/runtime payloads, offline/static-host assumptions, CSP/cache semantics, and viewer startup behavior.

Acceptance:
- Exported bundles behave correctly in the environments legacy users rely on.
- No existing export capability is removed or weakened.
- Root causes and proof obligations are recorded clearly.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:15.353993659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:36.551075321Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bundle","export","implementation","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:36.550288954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:15.353993659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.2","title":"Fix preview, watch, and pages-wizard workflow mismatches surfaced by the integration audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete fixes from `bd-2bpl.4.2` for operator-facing pages workflows, including happy path and failure path behavior.

Acceptance:
- Export/preview/watch/wizard flows are at least as usable as the legacy workflows.
- Failure handling gives operators enough context to recover without confusion.
- Saved config repair and lifecycle behaviors are explicitly covered.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:15.657556684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:37.206532769Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","pages","parity","preview","watch","wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:37.205717418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:15.657556684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -5,6 +5,11 @@ Legend:
 - `partial`: subset implemented.
 - `planned`: not implemented yet.
 
+Important reading note:
+- Robot/CLI parity claims below are generally backed by fixture and integration evidence.
+- Interactive TUI entries are intentionally stricter: current Rust screens are functional and heavily tested for stability, but the major operator-facing modes remain under an active FrankenTUI-first redesign program (`bd-2bpl.3.6.*`).
+- Snapshot/keyflow stability is not, by itself, enough to claim product-quality TUI parity.
+
 ## Robot / CLI
 | Legacy Capability | Status | Notes |
 |---|---|---|
@@ -26,12 +31,12 @@ Legend:
 | Legacy Capability | Status | Notes |
 |---|---|---|
 | Bare command launches TUI | complete | `bvr` launches frankentui app. |
-| Main list/detail split | complete | 42%/58% split, FocusPane::List/Detail toggle, Tab switching, full navigation in all 11 view modes. |
-| Board view (`b`) | complete | Lane-aware grouping with counts/bar charts, h/l/H/L/1-4/0/$ lane navigation, j/k within-lane paging, s grouping cycle (status/priority/type), e empty-lane toggle, / search with n/N, J/K dep nav in detail, box-drawn card rendering. |
-| Insights view (`i`) | complete | 10 cycling panels (s/S), bottleneck/critical-path/cycle/flow/hotspot metrics, e explanation toggle, x calculation proof, h/l pane focus, / search with n/N, J/K dep nav. |
-| Graph view (`g`) | complete | 3-section metrics panel (Importance/Flow & Connectivity/Connections) with 8 metrics, mini-bars, rank badges, in-degree/out-degree, cycle membership; ego-node ASCII art, BLOCKED BY / BLOCKS sections, Top PageRank list; h/l/H/L navigation, / search with n/N, J/K dep nav. |
-| History view (`h`) | complete | Lifecycle timeline pane with box-drawing tree connectors and lifecycle icons; milestones section shows created/claimed/closed/reopened with author; commit detail with type icons, author initials badges, file change breakdown with action icons and +/- stats; git-mode detail with COMMIT DETAILS/RELATED BEADS sections; keybinding hints in detail footer; responsive width breakpoints (Narrow/Medium/Wide split ratios); file tree panel with j/k navigation, Enter toggle/filter, Tab 3-way focus cycling; `o` open-in-browser, `y` copy-to-clipboard; `/` search with n/N match cycling in both bead and git modes. |
-| Full keybinding parity | complete | All keybindings from the matrix below are implemented with unit coverage: global (`?/Tab/Esc/q/Ctrl+C/j/k/Ctrl+d/u/Page/Home/End/G/o/c/r/a`), main (`s/b/i/g/h`), board (`h/l/H/L/1-4/0/$/j/k/Ctrl+d/u/s/e/J/K//`), graph (`h/l/H/L/Ctrl+d/u/J/K//`), insights (`h/l/s/S/e/x/J/K//`), history (`v/c/g/J/K/Enter//` with 5 search modes: all/commit/SHA/bead/author). Additional Rust-only modes: Actionable (`a`), Attention (`!`), Tree (`T`), LabelDashboard (`[`), FlowMatrix (`]`), TimeTravelDiff (`t`), Sprint (`S`). |
+| Main list/detail split | partial | Functional split-pane triage surface exists with navigation and focus switching, but the current main screen is still more report-like than cockpit-grade. Active redesign: `bd-2bpl.3.6.6`, `bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.6.6.3`. |
+| Board view (`b`) | partial | Board mode exists with grouping, navigation, search, and detail behavior, but the current presentation and interaction model are not yet at credible kanban-workspace parity. Active redesign: `bd-2bpl.3.6.7`, `bd-2bpl.3.6.7.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.6.7.2`. |
+| Insights view (`i`) | partial | Insights mode exposes real analytics and explanation toggles, but current information density and visual hierarchy still read more like rotating text panels than a high-confidence analytics console. Active redesign: `bd-2bpl.3.6.9`, `bd-2bpl.3.6.9.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.6.9.2`. |
+| Graph view (`g`) | partial | Graph mode has meaningful metrics and traversal, but it still behaves more like a metric report plus ego box than a true topology-exploration surface. Active redesign: `bd-2bpl.3.6.8`, `bd-2bpl.3.6.8.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.6.8.2`. |
+| History view (`h`) | partial | History mode is one of the stronger current screens and includes timeline/file-tree/search/jump workflows, but it still falls short of the intended investigation-workspace quality bar. Active redesign: `bd-2bpl.3.6.10`, `bd-2bpl.3.6.10.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.6.10.2`. |
+| Full keybinding parity | partial | The current Rust TUI implements a very large keybinding set with extensive tests, but key availability alone is not sufficient evidence of workflow-quality parity while the major mode redesign beads remain open. |
 
 ## TUI Fidelity Contract
 
@@ -148,11 +153,11 @@ Legend:
 5. **Confirm dialog**: `ModalOverlay::Confirm` → title+message overlay → `Y` accepts, `N`/`Esc` rejects.
 6. **Pages wizard**: `ModalOverlay::PagesWizard` → 4-step flow (export dir → title → options → review) → `Enter` advances, `Esc` cancels, `Backspace` goes back, `c`/`h` toggle options.
 
-### Remaining Fidelity Gaps
-- ~~Responsive width breakpoints~~ — **Implemented** (Narrow/Medium/Wide breakpoint enum with adaptive split ratios).
-- ~~File tree panel and `o`/`y` hotkeys~~ — **Implemented** (j/k navigation, Enter toggle/filter, Tab focus cycling, o open-in-browser, y copy-to-clipboard).
-- ~~Legacy history-specific search modes~~ — **Implemented** (HistorySearchMode enum with all 5 modes: All/Commit/Sha/Bead/Author; Tab cycles modes in search input).
-- ~~No snapshot-based automated visual regression framework yet.~~ — **Implemented** (55 insta snapshots + 25 keyflow journeys + 5 e2e multi-mode journey tests with artifact capture).
+### Active TUI Product Gaps
+- The current TUI is still architecturally closer to a string-heavy report renderer than to the intended FrankenTUI-driven operator console.
+- Main, board, graph, insights, and history all need mode-specific redesign work before product-quality parity should be claimed.
+- Current snapshot/keyflow coverage proves stability of the present implementation; it does not by itself prove the redesigned product bar.
+- Mouse/hit-region behavior, realistic scenario datasets, and richer E2E artifact logging are now tracked explicitly under `bd-2bpl.3.6.12.*` because they were previously too easy to under-prove.
 - Workspace auto-discovery defaults (Go-style implicit `.bv/workspace.yaml` discovery from workspace root, nested subdirs, and `--repo-path`) are implemented and covered by resolver + e2e tests.
 
 ## Integrations
@@ -169,6 +174,11 @@ Legend:
 | Fixture-driven parity tests | complete | Legacy fixture-backed conformance checks for diff/history/forecast/triage/plan/priority/burndown with adversarial coverage; edge-case fixtures; 89-issue `stress_complex_89.jsonl` stress fixture; **large-dataset stress fixtures**: `stress_large_500.jsonl` (500 issues, 7 topologies), `pathological_deps.jsonl` (233 issues, extreme dep patterns: deep chain, convergence/divergence, overlapping cycles, self-dep, bidirectional, long cycle, dangling), `malformed_metadata.jsonl` (24 issues, edge-case metadata); 49 stress tests in `tests/stress_fixtures.rs`; fixture catalog at `tests/testdata/FIXTURES.md`. |
 | Bench harness | complete | Criterion benchmark for triage path added. |
 
+TUI verification reality:
+- Current Rust TUI snapshots, keyflow tests, and journeys are valuable stability checks for the current implementation.
+- They should not be read as proof that major TUI modes have already reached legacy-quality operator parity.
+- The active redesign proof contract lives under `bd-2bpl.3.6.12.*` and requires screen-family proof, hit-region proof, realistic scenario datasets, structured artifact bundles, and replayable shell-level journeys before final TUI parity claims should return to `complete`.
+
 ### How to Rerun the Proof Set
 
 ```bash
@@ -393,6 +403,11 @@ Previously listed as excluded but now implemented:
 
 ## Phased Implementation Plan
 
+Historical note:
+- The wave table below records what landed in Rust, not the current product-quality verdict for the interactive TUI.
+- For robot/CLI and export surfaces, these gates are still a useful completion history.
+- For TUI-facing gates, implementation history must now be read alongside the active redesign subtree (`bd-2bpl.3.6.*`) and the stricter verification contract above.
+
 ### Wave 0: Spec Contract (COMPLETE)
 | Gate | Bead | Verification | Status |
 |---|---|---|---|

+| History view implementation milestone (responsive, file tree, search, o/y) | bd-33w.3.3 | Implemented features exist in Rust, but product-quality history parity is now tracked as `partial` pending `bd-2bpl.3.6.10.*` |

-| ~~Modal/wizard parity~~ | bd-33w.3.4 | **Implemented** — ModalOverlay enum (Tutorial, Confirm, PagesWizard), 4-step pages wizard, generic confirm dialog; `cargo test modal` passes (8 tests) |
-| ~~TUI regression harness (snapshots + keyflow)~~ | bd-33w.3.5 | **Implemented** — 21 insta snapshot tests (5 modes × 3 breakpoints + 6 post-interaction) + 11 keyflow journey tests; `cargo test snap_` and `cargo test keyflow_` pass |

 | ~~Docs hardened, roadmap self-contained~~ | bd-33w.8.1 | **Implemented** — README.md updated with full command surface (50+ commands), TUI key map table (11 view modes), test suite table (1,248 tests across 7 suites), CI workflow docs, benchmark docs; PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md updated with all 19 analysis modules, conformance/bench design, TUI fidelity status |
@@ -499,12 +514,12 @@ Prerequisites: Wave 3 complete.
 
 | Surface | Evidence | Status | Risk |
 |---|---|---|---|
-| **11 view modes** | `cargo test snap_` (55 snapshots) | PASS | None |
-| **Keyboard interaction** | `cargo test keyflow_` (25 journey tests) | PASS | None |
-| **E2E TUI journeys** | `cargo test e2e_journey_` (5 multi-mode flows with artifacts) | PASS | None |
+| **11 current view modes render and snapshot stably** | `cargo test snap_` (55 snapshots) | PASS | Stability evidence only; not a product-parity verdict |
+| **Keyboard interaction stays regression-tested** | `cargo test keyflow_` (25 journey tests) | PASS | Interaction stability only; major modes still under redesign |
+| **E2E TUI journeys run on the current implementation** | `cargo test e2e_journey_` (5 multi-mode flows with artifacts) | PASS | Current-implementation evidence only; redesign proof contract is stricter |
 | **Debug render** | `cargo test --test e2e_robot_matrix e2e_debug_render` | PASS | None |
-| **Modal/wizard flows** | `cargo test modal` (8 tests) | PASS | None |
-| **History view** | `cargo test history` (56 tests) | PASS | None |
+| **Modal/wizard flows** | `cargo test modal` (8 tests) | PASS | Modal infrastructure exists; broader shell/TUI product parity still open |
+| **History view current implementation stays tested** | `cargo test history` (56 tests) | PASS | Strongest current TUI slice, but still partial pending redesign |
 
 ### Quality Gates
 
@@ -530,10 +545,12 @@ Prerequisites: Wave 3 complete.
 
 ### Go/No-Go Decision
 
-All core robot commands, export surfaces, TUI interactions, pages wizard, and quality gates are passing with 1,318 tests (936 lib + 382 integration/conformance/e2e). The project is ready for release as a functional replacement for the legacy Go `bv` binary for all automated (robot) and interactive (TUI) workflows.
+All core robot commands, export surfaces, pages workflows, and quality gates are passing with 1,318 tests (936 lib + 382 integration/conformance/e2e). The project is in strong shape for automated/robot workflows and related export surfaces.
+
+The interactive TUI should not currently be described as a finished product-quality replacement for the legacy Go `bv` TUI. It is functional and test-stable, but the major operator-facing modes remain under the active FrankenTUI-first redesign and proof program (`bd-2bpl.3.6.*`).
 
 ## Open Gaps to 100%
-1. ~~Remaining TUI interaction parity (responsive history layout, file tree panel, `o`/`y` keys, search modes)~~ — **Done**.
-2. ~~54 missing CLI flags across correlation/impact, file analysis, label analytics, search, export, script, baseline, feedback, workflow, and metadata categories.~~ — **Done** (all surfaces implemented).
-3. ~~Interactive pages wizard, preview server, watch-export~~ — **Done** (9-step wizard with config persistence, preview with live reload, watch with debounce; 75 wizard + 20 e2e tests).
-4. No outstanding workspace auto-discovery gap — implicit `.bv/workspace.yaml` discovery is implemented and covered by resolver + e2e tests.
+1. Active TUI redesign across main, board, graph, insights, and history so the product reaches legacy-quality operator confidence before parity returns to `complete`.
+2. Active redesign proof contract: screen-family proof, hit-region proof, realistic scenario datasets, structured artifact bundles, and replayable shell-level journeys under `bd-2bpl.3.6.12.*`.

+Current project reality:
+- Robot/CLI parity is far ahead and is backed by fixture-heavy regression coverage.
+- The interactive TUI is functional, but it is not yet at credible product parity with legacy `bv`.
+- The active TUI goal is parity-first-then-beyond: recover legacy operator confidence and look/feel with fuller FrankenTUI usage, then exceed it with Rust-native improvements once the evidence exists.
+
 > **Note:** `asupersync` is declared as an optional Cargo dependency (`asupersync-runtime` feature gate) but is not used at runtime. It is reserved as a post-parity enhancement path for structured async orchestration (watcher pipelines, background index builds). The current async needs (background metric computation, file reload) are handled directly with standard library primitives. Go's `bv` has no equivalent async framework, so this is not a parity requirement.
